commands.review_summary:
The PR updates the Memory Wiki plugin and wiki CLI docs to describe `sources/` as structured source-page wrappers with frontmatter rather than arbitrary bare Markdown storage.

PR surface: Docs +9. Total +9 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Bare `sources/*.md` files are still parsed as source pages, and current lint reports missing identity, page type, and freshness metadata.

target.title: [codex] docs(memory-wiki): clarify sources wrapper contract
target.body:
## Summary

Closes #69700.

Clarifies the Memory Wiki `sources/` contract so the docs match the current
lint, ingest, compile, and search behavior:

- describe `sources/` as structured source-page wrappers, not arbitrary raw
  Markdown storage
- explain that source-page frontmatter keeps identity, page type, freshness,
  and provenance while the body preserves imported content
- mirror the same wording in the `wiki ingest` CLI docs

## Why

The existing plugin docs described `sources/` as "imported raw material", which
made it sound safe to place bare Markdown files there. Current Memory Wiki lint
expects source pages to carry frontmatter, so the docs needed to clarify that
raw imported content is preserved inside structured source pages.

## Verification

- `node scripts/docs-list.js`
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check`
- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs README.md`
- `node scripts/check-docs-i18n-glossary.mjs`
- `node scripts/docs-link-audit.mjs`
- `git diff --check`

I also tried `corepack pnpm docs:list`, but pnpm attempted to reconcile/remove
the existing `node_modules` tree in this checkout and aborted due to no TTY. I
did not allow dependency cleanup to continue. I tried the repo's documented
`lint:docs` path through `markdownlint-cli2`, but this checkout does not contain
`config/markdownlint-cli2.jsonc`; running default markdownlint on the two edited
files reported pre-existing file-level/table line issues outside this diff.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Memory Wiki documentation now tells users that `sources/`
contains structured source-page wrappers with required frontmatter, while raw
imported content is preserved in the page body.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch
`fix-memory-wiki-sources-docs`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/docs-list.js`; `node
scripts/format-docs.mjs --check`; `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs
README.md`; `node scripts/check-docs-i18n-glossary.mjs`; `node
scripts/docs-link-audit.mjs`; `git diff --check`.

Evidence after fix: Docs list completed, docs formatting reported clean, docs
MDX check passed, docs i18n glossary check exited successfully, link audit
reported `broken_links=0`, and `git diff --check` exited successfully.

Observed result after fix: The Memory Wiki plugin and CLI docs now describe
source pages as structured wrappers with frontmatter for identity, page type,
freshness, and provenance.

What was not tested: Full `pnpm check:docs` was not run because pnpm attempted
dependency reconciliation in this checkout; markdownlint with the repo config
was not run because `config/markdownlint-cli2.jsonc` is absent here.
